content.cn_text: 你将听到一段描述审判法庭和法律审判过程的录音。
content.en_text: You will hear a recording describing a trial courtroom and the conduct of legal trials.

content.cn_text: 在播放录音之前，你有一些时间阅读问题31-40。
content.en_text: First you have some time to look at questions 31-40.

content.cn_text: [暂停1分钟]
content.en_text: [Pause 1 minute]

content.cn_text: 现在仔细听录音，回答问题31-40。
content.en_text: Now listen carefully and answer questions 31-40.

content.cn_text: 虽然法庭布局不同，但这是对标准英国审判法庭的描述。
content.en_text: Although variations occur in courtroom layouts, this is a description of a standard UK trial courtroom.

content.cn_text: 在审判法庭上，法官总是坐在高架的桌子或长凳后面。与长凳相邻的是证人席和一个稍大的部分，以容纳法庭记者。
content.en_text: In a trial courtroom, the judge is invariably seated behind an elevated desk or bench.Adjacent to the bench are the witness stand and a section that is slightly larger to accommodate the  court reporter.

content.cn_text: 在法庭记录员的后面，法警在整个诉讼过程中都靠着法庭的墙站着。
content.en_text: Behind the court reporter, the bailiff stands against the wall of the courtroom throughout the proceedings.

content.cn_text: 他的工作是确保法庭程序得到遵守和维持秩序。
content.en_text: His job is to ensure the court procedure is observed and order is maintained.

content.cn_text: 在法官席的正前方，位于被称为“井”的法庭中央区域，有一张桌子，法庭书记员坐在那里记录法庭程序。
content.en_text: Directly in front of the judge's bench, located in the central courtroom area known as 'the well', Is a table where the court clerk sits and records court proceedings.

content.cn_text: 只有法警被授权在开庭期间穿过这口井，在律师和法官之间传递文件。
content.en_text: Only the bailiff is authorised to cross the well during a court session to transfer documents between the lawyers and the judge.

content.cn_text: 任何未经许可而过井的行为都被视为对法庭极不尊重，通常是明令禁止的。
content.en_text: Any unauthorised crossing of the well is regarded as being extremely disrespectful to the court and is usually expressly forbidden.

content.cn_text: 在书记员办公桌的一边有一张桌子，被告和他的律师坐在那里。
content.en_text: Off to one side of the clerk's desk Is located a table where the defendant and his lawyer are seated.

content.cn_text: 被告，也被称为“被告”与他的律师密切合作，他的律师也被称为辩护律师。
content.en_text: The defendant, also known as 'the accused' works closely with his lawyer who is also termed the lawyer for the defence.

content.cn_text: 在法庭的另一边，控方律师与原告坐在一起，原告将对被告提起诉讼。
content.en_text: Across the other side of the courtroom, the lawyer for the prosecution sits with the plaintiff, who is bringing the case against the defendant to court.

content.cn_text: 旁边是陪审团包厢，它占据了整个法庭的最大面积。
content.en_text: Close by Is the jury box that covers the largest area in the courtroom.

content.cn_text: 它有12名公众成员，他们负责对被告作出最终判决。
content.en_text: It holds 12 members of the public who are responsible for delivering the final verdict on the defendant.

content.cn_text: 所有积极参与法庭诉讼的人都在法庭中被称为“吧台”的区域。
content.en_text: All those who are actively involved In court proceedings are in the area of the courtroom termed 'the bar'.

content.cn_text: 酒吧后面是旁听席，旁听席上的观众可能是参与庭审的人的亲属，也可能只是好奇的公众人士。
content.en_text: Behind the area of the bar is the gallery which seats spectators who may be relatives of those involved in the court hearing or merely curious members of the public.

content.cn_text: 酒吧本身可能是一个实际的物理屏障，如栏杆或仅仅是一个指定的区域。
content.en_text: The bar itself may be an actual physical barrier, such as a railing or merely a designated area.

content.cn_text: 除了案件当事人和证人之外，只有律师才能真正通过律师资格考试(法庭人员和陪审团成员通常从法官席后面的不同门进入)，这就是为什么“律师资格”一词用来指律师
content.en_text: Apart from the parties to the case and any witnesses, only the lawyers can literally pass the bar (court personnel and jury members usually enter through separate doors behind the bench) and this is why the term 'the bar' has come to refer to

content.cn_text: 法律职业作为一个整体。
content.en_text: the legal profession as a whole.

content.cn_text: 这就是法庭的设置。
content.en_text: So, that is the court set-up.

content.cn_text: 现在来看看法官是如何做出判决和宣判的。
content.en_text: Now onto how the verdict is arrived at and sentence proclaimed by the judge.

content.cn_text: 当所有证据都由控方律师和辩方律师提供并提出质疑后，就可以开始作出判决。
content.en_text: When all evidence has been given and challenged, by both the lawyer for the prosecution and the lawyer for the defence, then the process of reaching a verdict can begin.

content.cn_text: 在法官总结案件之前，法官首先会见双方律师是正常的法庭程序。
content.en_text: Prior to the judge's summing up the case, it is normal court procedure for the judge to meet with both lawyers first.

content.cn_text: 在会议上，法官将说什么是由律师和法官双方同意决定的。
content.en_text: In the meeting, what the judge will say is determined by mutual consent between the lawyers and the judge.

content.cn_text: 然后由法官决定是否将总结分成两部分。
content.en_text: It is then up to the judge to decide if the summing up will be split into two parts.

content.cn_text: 如果采取这种做法，总结将分为法律部分和案件事实摘要。
content.en_text: If this course of action is taken, the summing up will be divided into a legal part and a summary of the facts of the case.

content.cn_text: 法律部分基本上是澄清指控是什么，必须证明什么，以及对案件的任何特别指示，例如需要尊重保密。
content.en_text: The legal part is basically a clarification of what the charge is and what has to be proven in addition to any special directives for the case, such as the need to respect confidentiality.

content.cn_text: 摘要是为了确保所有陪审团成员都能了解案件的基本事实。
content.en_text: The summary is given to ensure that all members of the jury are reminded of the essential facts of the case.

content.cn_text: 这是陪审团在审判期间听取的所有证据的公正总结。
content.en_text: It is an impartial summary of all the evidence heard during the trial by the jury.

content.cn_text: 总结后，法官将强调达成一致判决的重要性。
content.en_text: After the summing up, the judge will stress the Importance of reaching a unanimous verdict.

content.cn_text: 然后陪审团将退庭考虑他们的决定。
content.en_text: The jury will then retire to consider their decision.

content.cn_text: 如果案件特别复杂，法官将首先向陪审团发出一份题为“判决路线”的书面文件。
content.en_text: If the case is particularly complex, the judge will first issue the jury with a written document entitled a ' route to verdict'.

content.cn_text: 这本质上是陪审团在考虑判决时应该提出的一系列问题。
content.en_text: This is essentially a series of questions the jury should pose themselves whilst considering the verdict.

content.cn_text: 陪审团作出裁决所需的时间从几个小时到几天不等。
content.en_text: The time taken for a jury to reach a verdict can vary from a few hours to several days.

content.cn_text: 如果有可能在开庭日结束前无法做出判决，那么陪审团成员可以自由回家，但附带条件是他们不得在法庭外与任何人讨论案件的细节。
content.en_text: If there is a chance that the verdict will not be reached, before the end of the court day, then members of the jury are free to go home on the proviso that they will not discuss details of the case with anyone outside the courtroom.

content.cn_text: 案件越严重，陪审团作出裁决的时间可能越长。如果陪审团继续努力达成裁决，法官将发出“沃森指示”，也被称为“给予和接受指示”。从本质上讲，这是一项指示，提醒陪审团他们的誓言，并在讨论判决时保持公正的观点。
content.en_text: The more serious the case, the longer a jury may take to reach a decision, If the jury continues to struggle to reach a verdict, the judge will deliver a 'Watson Direction' which is also known as a 'give and take direction'.Essentially, this is a directive reminding the jury of their oath and to maintain unbiased views whilst discussing the verdict.

content.cn_text: 如果陪审团不能达成一致意见，则允许多数裁决。
content.en_text: Should the jury not be unanimous in their decision,then a majority verdict is permitted.

content.cn_text: 一旦作出判决，对被告的判决就完全交给法官了。
content.en_text: Once the verdict has been delivered, the sentencing of the accused is left entirely to- the judge.

content.cn_text: 然而，陪审团被允许事先影响法官的判决，以使其更加宽大。
content.en_text: The jury is, however, permitted beforehand to influence the judge's sentencing in order to make it more lenient.

content.cn_text: 因此，在陪审团的裁决中增加了“宽恕建议”，法官是否考虑到这一点完全取决于他们。
content.en_text: Accordingly, a 'recommendation of mercy' is added to the jury verdict, Whether the judge takes this Into account is entirely up to them.

content.cn_text: 旁白:这是第四部分的结尾。
content.en_text: Narrator: That is the end of Part 4.

content.cn_text: 你现在有半分钟的时间检查答案。
content.en_text: You now have half a minute to check your answers.

content.cn_text: [暂停30秒]
content.en_text: [Pause 30 seconds]

content.cn_text: 听力测试到此结束。
content.en_text: That is the end of the listening test.

content.cn_text: 你现在有十分钟的时间把答案誊写在听力答题卡上。
content.en_text: You now have ten minutes to transfer your answers to the Listening Answer Sheet.

groups.desc:
Questions 31-36
Label the diagram below.
Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS for each answer.

items.analysis: 题干要求标记图中第31的位置，定位词是“Judge's bench”，在听力原文中对应的信号词是“bench”。听力原文中提到“Adjacent to the bench are the witness stand and a section that is slightly larger to accommodate the court reporter.”，这句话明确指出在法官席旁边的是证人席（witness stand）和法院记录员的区域。由于图中第31的位置在法官席旁边，因此答案是“witness stand”。学生需要注意听力材料中关于位置关系的描述，特别是“adjacent to the bench”这样的信号词，以准确定位答案。

因为原文提到“Adjacent to the bench are... a section... to accommodate the court reporter.”，说明在法官的桌子(bench)旁边有一个区域是给书记员(court reporter)用的，所以第32题的正确答案是“court reporter”。图中显示的32题空格位置正好是法官旁边的区域，因此填“court reporter"是正确的。“Judge's bench”是定位词，对应原文中的“bench”，并且“Adjacent to the bench”作为信号词提示我们答案位置在此附近。

题目中的定位词是“33”和“stands against the wall”，在听力原文中对应的信号词是“Behind the court reporter”和“bailiff”。原文句子“Behind the court reporter, the bailiff stands against the wall of the courtroom throughout the proceedings.”明确指出了法警的位置是在记录员的后面，靠墙站立。因此，正确答案是“bailiff”。学生需要注意听力中的位置描述和角色职责，以便准确定位答案。

文中提到 "Directly in front of the judge's bench, located in the central courtroom area known as 'the well', is a table where the court clerk sits and records court proceedings." 这句话明确指出了书记员的位置是在法官席位的正前方，位于法庭中心区域。因此，答案是 "court clerk"。学生需要注意定位词 "central courtroom area" 和信号词 "in front of the judge's bench"，这些词帮助我们准确定位到书记员的位置。
因为文中提到“jury box”是法庭中最大的区域，并且容纳了12名公众成员，这些成员负责对被告作出最终判决，所以可以确定第35题的位置是“jury box”。
文中明确提到 gallery 坐满了 spectators，因此这个区域的答案为 spectators。通过定位词 gallery 和 seats，在听力中找到相应的描述，并且这个描述中明确指出了答案。

groups.desc:
Questions 37-40
Complete the flow chart below.
Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS for each answer.
groups.collect:
The process of reaching a court verdict
Once all court evidence is given and examined, the judge will summarise the case.
Usually the contents of the summary are $14206$ a meeting between the judge and the lawyers for the defence and for the prosecution.
The summing up may be split into two sections: a legal component and a summary.
In the former section, legal procedure is outlined and $14207$ is given of the accusation being made against the defendant.
In the latter section, the judge tries to ensure $14208$ summing up of the case.
The judge will stress the importance of reaching a unanimous verdict to the jury.
Before the jury retires, the judge may offer written guidelines, known as the ' $14209$ to verdict'.

items.analysis: 这道题的关键在于理解法庭程序中法官总结案情的过程。文中提到，在法官进行总结之前，会先与律师们进行会面。在这个会面中，法官将要说的内容是由律师和法官共同决定的。这就是为什么答案是"determined by"。这个短语准确地表达了法官总结内容的决定过程。注意，虽然原文中有"mutual consent"这个短语，但题目要求答案不超过两个词，所以我们只能选择"determined by"。这个答案不仅符合题目要求，还准确反映了法官总结内容的决定机制。记住，在做这类题目时，要注意听力材料中的关键词和短语，特别是那些描述程序或决策过程的词语。

这道题的正确答案是"clarification"，因为文中明确指出法律部分主要是对指控内容的澄清。注意题干中的"legal component"和"accusation"这两个关键词，它们在原文中分别对应"The legal part"和"charge"。"Clarification"这个词在法律语境中更为准确，它不仅仅是简单的描述或解释，而是对指控内容的明确阐述。此外，这个词也符合题目要求的"NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS"。在做这类题目时，要特别注意法律术语的准确使用，并且要能够快速捕捉听力材料中的关键信息。记住，在法庭上，对指控内容的明确说明是非常重要的，所以"clarification"是最合适的答案。

这道题的关键在于理解法官的总结必须是公正的。文中明确指出总结是"impartial"（公正的），这与题目要求的"ensure ... summing up"完全吻合。"impartial"一词在法律语境中非常重要，因为它强调了法官在总结案情时必须保持中立，不偏不倚。这确保了陪审团在做出判决时能够基于公正、客观的信息。此外，注意"summary"是"summing up"的同义替换，这是定位答案的关键。作为学生，你应该关注这类法律程序中的关键词，它们往往直接反映了司法系统的核心原则。记住，在听力考试中，同义词替换是常见的考点，培养识别同义表达的能力对提高成绩很有帮助。

这道题的答案是"route"，因为原文中明确提到了"route to verdict"这个法律术语。这里有几个关键点需要注意：首先，题干中提到的"written guidelines"在原文中对应的是"written document"，这是一个很好的定位点。其次，这个文件的具体名称是"route to verdict"，正好符合题目所需填写的空白。"Route"在这里的含义是"路线"或"途径"，表示引导陪审团达成判决的过程。这个术语在法律领域是特定的，所以不能用其他近义词如"path"或"way"替代。另外，请注意原文中"If the case is particularly complex"这个条件，说明这种文件并不是在所有案件中都会使用，而是在复杂案件中才会出现。理解这个上下文可以帮助你更好地把握答案的准确性。
